      57                 :            : /*
      58                 :            :  * A struct to hold all the data relevant to a value, used for
      59                 :            :  * buffering.
      60                 :            :  */
      61                 :            : typedef struct WindowValue
      62                 :            : {
      63                 :            :         /*
      64                 :            :          * Total number of not NULL argument for the function associated with this
      65                 :            :          * value.
      66                 :            :          *
      67                 :            :          * This is only used for aggregate functions that have inverse preliminary
      68                 :            :          * functions. For this kind of functions, when their argument values are
      69                 :            :          * all NULLs in the current frame, their result value should be NULL as
      70                 :            :          * well. This value is used to tell if all argument values in the current
      71                 :            :          * frame are all NULLs.
      72                 :            :          */
      73                 :            :         uint64          numNotNulls;
      74                 :            :         Datum           value;
      75                 :            :         bool            valueIsNull;
      76                 :            : }       WindowValue;
      77                 :            : 
      78                 :            : /*
      79                 :            :  * A higher-level structure to represent an entry in the frame buffer.
      80                 :            :  */
      81                 :            : typedef struct FrameBufferEntry
      82                 :            : {
      83                 :            :         /*
      84                 :            :          * Keys for RANGE frames.
      85                 :            :          *
      86                 :            :          * This is only used for RANGE frames. For ROWS frame, these values are NULL.
      87                 :            :          */
      88                 :            :         Datum      *keys;
      89                 :            :         bool       *nulls;
      90                 :            : 
      91                 :            :         /*
      92                 :            :          * A list of intermediate function values to be buffered in the frame
      93                 :            :          * buffer. Each of values is of WindowValue type.
      94                 :            :          */
      95                 :            :         List       *func_values;
      96                 :            : } FrameBufferEntry;

     101                 :            : typedef struct WindowStatePerLevelData
     102                 :            : {
     103                 :            :         Index           level;                  /* index of WindowKey for this level */
     104                 :            :         FmgrInfo   *eqfunctions;        /* equality fns for partial key */
     105                 :            :         FmgrInfo   *ltfunctions;        /* less-than functions for partial key */
     106                 :            :         bool            need_peercount;
     107                 :            : 
     108                 :            :         int64           group_index;
     109                 :            :         int64           prior_non_peer_count;
     110                 :            :         int64           peer_index;
     111                 :            :         int64           peer_count;
     112                 :            :         int64           rank;
     113                 :            :         int64           dense_rank;
     114                 :            :         int64           prior_rank;
     115                 :            :         int64           prior_dense_rank;
     116                 :            : 
     117                 :            :         /* List of functions in this key level */
     118                 :            :         List       *level_funcs;
     119                 :            : 
     120                 :            :         /* The framing clause */
     121                 :            :         WindowFrame *frame;
     122                 :            : 
     123                 :            :         /* Indicate if all functions have trivial frames. */
     124                 :            :         bool            trivial_frames_only;
     125                 :            : 
     126                 :            :         /* if the user didn't specify a frame, we use the default */
     127                 :            :         bool            default_frame;
     128                 :            : 
     129                 :            :         /* Indicate if the frame is a ROW frame or a RANGE frame. */
     130                 :            :         bool            is_rows;
     131                 :            : 
     132                 :            :         /*
     133                 :            :          * Indicate if the frame clause for this level has an edge of DELAY_BOUND
     134                 :            :          * type.
     135                 :            :          */
     136                 :            :         bool            has_delay_bound;
     137                 :            : 
     138                 :            :         /*
     139                 :            :          * Indicate if all functions in this level are aggregate functions that
     140                 :            :          * have no preliminary functions or inverse preliminary functions.
     141                 :            :          */
     142                 :            :         bool            has_only_trans_funcs;
     143                 :            : 
     144                 :            :         /* user can specify ascending or descending order, record it here */
     145                 :            :         bool       *col_sort_asc;
     146                 :            : 
     147                 :            :         /*
     148                 :            :          * The partial order by keys. There is at least one sort key in this
     149                 :            :          * array. It is used for convenience to find the order by keys since we
     150                 :            :          * need to store them in the frame buffer for RANGE-based framing.
     151                 :            :          */

     153                 :            :         AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
     154                 :            :         Oid                *sortOperators;
     155                 :            : 
     156                 :            :         /* column type data */
     157                 :            :         Oid                *col_types;
     158                 :            :         int2       *col_typlens;
     159                 :            :         bool       *col_typbyvals;
     160                 :            : 
     161                 :            :         /*
     162                 :            :          * Frame is contradictory and no tuples will fall into the frame, for
     163                 :            :          * example: BETWEEN 10 FOLLOWING AND 10 PRECEDING.
     164                 :            :          */
     165                 :            :         bool            empty_frame;
     166                 :            : 
     167                 :            :         /* XXX: merge the following 4 fields into 2 */
     168                 :            :         /* state for rows frame is simple (for now) */
     169                 :            :         long int        trail_rows;
     170                 :            :         long int        lead_rows;
     171                 :            : 
     172                 :            :         /* state for range frame is more complex */
     173                 :            :         Datum           trail_range;
     174                 :            :         Datum           lead_range;
     175                 :            : 
     176                 :            :         /*
     177                 :            :          * The number of rows between the trailing edge and the current_row, and
     178                 :            :          * between the leading edge and the current_row.
     179                 :            :          *
     180                 :            :          * For preceding edges, these numbers are 0 or negative.
     181                 :            :          */
     182                 :            :         long int        num_trail_rows;
     183                 :            :         long int        num_lead_rows;
     184                 :            : 
     185                 :            :         /* state for frame edges (for both ROWS and RANGE frames) */
     186                 :            :         ExprState  *trail_expr;
     187                 :            :         ExprState  *lead_expr;
     188                 :            : 
     189                 :            :         /* state for RANGE-frame edge computation */

     191                 :            :         ExprState  *lead_range_expr;
     192                 :            : 
     193                 :            :         /* RANGE frame only; see comments on init_bound_frame_edge_expr */
     194                 :            :         ExprState  *trail_range_eq_expr;
     195                 :            :         ExprState  *lead_range_eq_expr;
     196                 :            : 
     197                 :            :         /*
     198                 :            :          * The frame buffer to buffer the intermediate results for the functions
     199                 :            :          * in this level key.
     200                 :            :          */
     201                 :            :         struct WindowFrameBufferData *frame_buffer;
     202                 :            : 
     203                 :            :         /*
     204                 :            :          * These two readers are pointing to the trailing and leading edges of
     205                 :            :          * this frame, respectively.
     206                 :            :          */
     207                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *trail_reader;
     208                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *lead_reader;
     209                 :            : 
     210                 :            :         /*
     211                 :            :          * Indicate if lead_reader is pointing to the exact edge position. With
     212                 :            :          * this flag, we don't need to do the tuple comparison again during
     213                 :            :          * checking if there are sufficient data to generate an output.
     214                 :            :          */
     215                 :            :         bool            lead_ready;
     216                 :            : 
     217                 :            :         /*
     218                 :            :          * Indicate if funcstate->aggTransValue actually contains the aggregate
     219                 :            :          * value.
     220                 :            :          */
     221                 :            :         bool            agg_filled;
     222                 :            : 
     223                 :            :         /*
     224                 :            :          * FrameBufferEntry buffers to avoid pallocs/pfrees.
     225                 :            :          */
     226                 :            :         FrameBufferEntry *curr_entry_buf;
     227                 :            :         FrameBufferEntry *trail_entry_buf;
     228                 :            :         FrameBufferEntry *lead_entry_buf;
     229                 :            :         
     230                 :            :         /* A char buffer to temporarily hold serialized data before
     231                 :            :         *  writing them to the frame buffer, and keep deserialized
     232                 :            :         *  data when reading from the frame buffer.
     233                 :            :         *
     234                 :            :         * Use this pre-allocated buffer to avoid doing
     235                 :            :         * palloc/pfree many times.
     236                 :            :         *
     237                 :            :         * The size of this array is specified by 'max_size'.
     238                 :            :         */
     239                 :            :         char *serial_array;
     240                 :            :         Size max_size;
     241                 :            : }       WindowStatePerLevelData;

     268                 :            :

     273                 :            :         Oid                     invtransfn_oid;

     275                 :            :

     279                 :            :         FmgrInfo        invtransfn;
     280                 :            :         FmgrInfo        invprelimfn;
     281                 :            : 
     282                 :            :         Datum           aggInitValue;
     283                 :            :         bool            aggInitValueIsNull;
     284                 :            : 
     285                 :            :         int16           aggTranstypeLen;
     286                 :            :         bool            aggTranstypeByVal;
     287                 :            : 
     288                 :            :         /* the intermediate transition value */
     289                 :            :         Datum           aggTransValue;
     290                 :            :         bool            aggTransValueIsNull;
     291                 :            :         bool            aggNoTransValue;
     292                 :            : 
     293                 :            :         /* the final transition value for output tuples */
     294                 :            :         Datum           final_aggTransValue;
     295                 :            :         bool            final_aggTransValueIsNull;
     296                 :            :         bool            final_aggNoTransValue;
     297                 :            : 
     298                 :            :         /*
     299                 :            :          * The final transition value may be a copy of a value stored in the frame
     300                 :            :          * buffre, or the above intermediate in-memory transition value. We should
     301                 :            :          * free the copied value. This boolean is used to determine if
     302                 :            :          * final_aggTransValue should be freed.
     303                 :            :          */
     304                 :            :         bool            final_aggShouldFree;
     305                 :            : 
     306                 :            :         /* frame does not require buffering and complexity of invokeWindowFuncs() */
     307                 :            :         bool            trivial_frame;
     308                 :            : 
     309                 :            :         /*
     310                 :            :          * The index for the intermediate value of this function when serializing
     311                 :            :          * its value along with others before storing them into frame buffers.
     312                 :            :          *
     313                 :            :          * The initial value is -1.
     314                 :            :          */
     315                 :            :         int                     serial_index;
     316                 :            : 
     317                 :            :         /*
     318                 :            :          * The total number of not NULL arguments for this function so far.
     319                 :            :          */
     320                 :            :         uint64          numNotNulls;
     321                 :            : }       WindowStatePerFunctionData;

     335                 :            : #define EDGE_IS_DELAYED(e) \

     346                 :            :           e->kind == WINDOW_DELAYED_BOUND_FOLLOWING)
     347                 :            : #define EDGE_EQ_CURRENT_ROW(level_state, wstate, e, is_lead) \

     351                 :            :                         ((!is_lead && \
     352                 :            :                            (((level_state)->is_rows && (level_state)->trail_rows == 0) || \
     353                 :            :                                 (!(level_state)->is_rows && exec_eq_exprstate((wstate), \

     356                 :            :                           (((level_state)->is_rows && (level_state)->lead_rows == 0) || \

     360                 :            : /****************************************************
     361                 :            :  * Window Input Buffer and its APIs
     362                 :            :  ***************************************************/
     363                 :            : /*
     364                 :            :  * WindowInputBuffer: this buffer holds input tuples that fit in the
     365                 :            :  * partition which is currently processed by the window node. This
     366                 :            :  * buffer also stores the first tuple in the next partition, which
     367                 :            :  * is always the last tuple in the buffer.
     368                 :            :  */
     369                 :            : typedef struct WindowInputBufferData
     370                 :            : {
     371                 :            :         NTupleStore *tuplestore;
     372                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *writer;
     373                 :            : 
     374                 :            :         /* The reader that is always pointed to the previous current_row */
     375                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *current_row_reader;
     376                 :            : 
     377                 :            :         /* The reader that reads tuples in the buffer */
     378                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *reader;
     379                 :            : 
     380                 :            :         /*
     381                 :            :          * Indicate if the last tuple in the buffer is the tuple that breaks the
     382                 :            :          * partition key.
     383                 :            :          */
     384                 :            :         bool            part_break;
     385                 :            : 
     386                 :            :         /* The total number of tuples in the store. */
     387                 :            :         int64           num_tuples;
     388                 :            : }       WindowInputBufferData;
     389                 :            : 
     390                 :            : static void resetInputBuffer(WindowState * wstate);
     391                 :            : static void freeInputBuffer(WindowState * wstate);
     392                 :            : static void trimInputBuffer(WindowInputBuffer buffer);
     393                 :            : 
     394                 :            : /********************************************************
     395                 :            :  * Window Frame Buffer and its APIs
     396                 :            :  *******************************************************/
     397                 :            : /*
     398                 :            :  * WindowFrameBuffer: this buffer holds intermediate aggregate values
     399                 :            :  * or input arguments in order to generate the final aggregates for
     400                 :            :  * the next output row.
     401                 :            :  *
     402                 :            :  * This buffer will trim old values when they are outside the lower bound
     403                 :            :  * of the given frame provided to this buffer. This frame can be a RANGE
     404                 :            :  * frame or a ROW frame. Note that if this frame is a RANGE frame, the
     405                 :            :  * actual number of values in the buffer may vary over time.
     406                 :            :  *
     407                 :            :  * This buffer is designed to be one for each key level.  We could
     408                 :            :  * also share one buffer among key levels if they have the same list
     409                 :            :  * functions.
     410                 :            :  */
     411                 :            : typedef struct WindowFrameBufferData
     412                 :            : {
     413                 :            :         NTupleStore *tuplestore;
     414                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *writer;
     415                 :            : 
     416                 :            :         /*
     417                 :            :          * This reader is used to scan through the buffer to compute the final
     418                 :            :          * result.
     419                 :            :          */
     420                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *reader;
     421                 :            : 
     422                 :            :         /* Indicate if this buffer is for a RANGE frame or a ROWS frame. */
     423                 :            :         bool            is_rows;
     424                 :            : 
     425                 :            :         /*
     426                 :            :          * The number of rows before and after the current_row. Note that
     427                 :            :          * 'num_rows_after' counts the current_row.
     428                 :            :          *
     429                 :            :          * These two counters are used to adjust frame edges for frames with
     430                 :            :          * expressions.
     431                 :            :          *
     432                 :            :          * Currently, these two counters do not be modified after a trim operation.
     433                 :            :          */
     434                 :            :         long int        num_rows_before;
     435                 :            :         long int        num_rows_after;
     436                 :            : 
     437                 :            :         /*
     438                 :            :          * The trailing and leading number of rows from current_row if this frame
     439                 :            :          * is a ROW frame.
     440                 :            :          */
     441                 :            :         long int        trail_rows;
     442                 :            :         long int        lead_rows;
     443                 :            : 
     444                 :            :         /*
     445                 :            :          * The trailing and leading range from current_row if this frame is a
     446                 :            :          * RANGE frame.
     447                 :            :          */
     448                 :            :         Datum           trail_range;
     449                 :            :         Datum           lead_range;
     450                 :            : 
     451                 :            :         /* The reader that always points to the current_row. */
     452                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *current_row_reader;
     453                 :            : 
     454                 :            :         /*
     455                 :            :          * The accessor that defines the point before which all values in the
     456                 :            :          * buffer can be trimmed.
     457                 :            :          */
     458                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *trim_reader;
     459                 :            : 
     460                 :            :         /*
     461                 :            :          * Pointer to the level state. Information about window functions can be
     462                 :            :          * found here.
     463                 :            :          */
     464                 :            :         WindowStatePerLevel level_state;
     465                 :            : }       WindowFrameBufferData;
     466                 :            : typedef WindowFrameBufferData *WindowFrameBuffer;
     467                 :            : 
     468                 :            : static WindowFrameBuffer createRangeFrameBuffer(Datum trail_range,
     469                 :            :                                            Datum lead_range,
     470                 :            :                                            int bytes);
     471                 :            : static WindowFrameBuffer createRowsFrameBuffer(long int trail_rows,
     472                 :            :                                           long int lead_rows,
     473                 :            :                                           int bytes);
     474                 :            : static WindowFrameBuffer resetFrameBuffer(WindowFrameBuffer buffer);
     475                 :            : static void appendToFrameBuffer(WindowStatePerLevel level_state,
     476                 :            :                                         WindowState * wstate,
     477                 :            :                                         bool last_peer);
     478                 :            : static void trimFrameBuffer(WindowFrameBuffer buffer);
     479                 :            : static void incrementCurrentRow(WindowFrameBuffer buffer, WindowState * wstate);
     480                 :            : static bool hasEnoughDataInRange(WindowFrameBuffer buffer,
     481                 :            :                                          WindowStatePerLevel level_state,
     482                 :            :                                          WindowState * wstate,
     483                 :            :                                          Datum tail_range,
     484                 :            :                                          Datum lead_range);
     485                 :            : static bool hasEnoughDataInRows(WindowFrameBuffer buffer,
     486                 :            :                                         WindowStatePerLevel level_state,
     487                 :            :                                         WindowState * wstate,
     488                 :            :                                         long int trail_rows,
     489                 :            :                                         long int lead_rows);
     490                 :            : static void computeFrameValue(WindowStatePerLevel level_state,
     491                 :            :                                   WindowState * wstate,
     492                 :            :                                   NTupleStoreAccessor * trail_reader,
     493                 :            :                                   NTupleStoreAccessor * lead_reader);
     494                 :            :

     502                 :            : /*
     503                 :            :  * WindowBufferCursor
     504                 :            :  * This is an abstract cursor to scan the frame buffer.  This holds transient
     505                 :            :  * state of a window level, so you have to make sure the scan on the same window
     506                 :            :  * level should happen once at a time.
     507                 :            :  */
     508                 :            : typedef struct WindowBufferCursorData
     509                 :            : {
     510                 :            :         WindowState *wstate;            /* state of this window */
     511                 :            :         WindowStatePerLevel level_state;        /* state of this window level */
     512                 :            :         NTupleStoreAccessor *reader;    /* reader to scan rows */
     513                 :            :         NTupleStorePos orig_pos;        /* original position of the reader */
     514                 :            :         bool            use_last_agg;   /* should scan "last_agg"? */
     515                 :            :         bool            reader_eof;             /* is reader at end of scan? */
     516                 :            :         bool            has_tuples;             /* does frame has tuples? */
     517                 :            :         bool            forward;                /* scan direction */
     518                 :            :         bool            first;                  /* is first fetch of tupstore? */
     519                 :            : }       WindowBufferCursorData;
     520                 :            : 
     521                 :            : typedef struct WindowBufferCursorData *WindowBufferCursor;
     522                 :            : 
     523                 :            : static WindowBufferCursor windowBufferBeginScan(WindowState * wstate,
     524                 :            :                                           WindowStatePerLevel level_state, bool forward);

     526                 :            : static List *windowBufferNextLastAgg(WindowBufferCursor cursor);
     527                 :            : static List *windowBufferNext(WindowBufferCursor cursor);
     528                 :            : static void windowBufferEndScan(WindowBufferCursor cursor);
     529                 :            :

     688                 :            : /*

     716                 :            :

     718                 :            :

     801                 :            : /*
     802                 :            :  * ensureSpace -- ensure that there is enough space in the buffer.

     804                 :            :  * This function returns the new written position if the array is

     807                 :            : static inline char *
     808                 :            : ensureSpace(char **p_serial_entry, Size * p_max_size,
     809                 :            :                         char *written_pos, Size written_len)

     822                 :            : /*
     823                 :            :  * serializeValue -- serialize a given value (Datum) into a char array.
     824                 :            :  *
     825                 :            :  * The start writing position in the given char array is specified by
     826                 :            :  * 'start_pos'. If this value is too big to fit into the available
     827                 :            :  * space in the array, the size of the array is increased in this
     828                 :            :  * function.
     829                 :            :  *
     830                 :            :  * The return pointer points to the next char position in the
     831                 :            :  * given char array after writing the serialized string of the
     832                 :            :  * given value.
     833                 :            :  */
     834                 :            : static char *
     835                 :            : serializeValue(Datum value, bool isnull,
     836                 :            :                            bool byvalue, int16 typelen,

     838                 :            :                            Size start_pos)

     840                 :     132692 :         char       *written_pos = (*p_serial_entry) + start_pos;
     841                 :            : 
     842                 :     132692 :         written_pos = ensureSpace(p_serial_entry, p_max_size,
     843                 :     132692 :                                                           written_pos, sizeof(bool));
     844                 :            : 
     845                 :     132692 :         memcpy(written_pos, &isnull, sizeof(bool));
     846                 :     132692 :         written_pos += sizeof(bool);    /* isnull col */

     850         [ +  + ]:     132651 :                 if (byvalue)

     855                 :     117027 :                         memcpy(written_pos, &value, sizeof(Datum));

     858                 :            :                 else
     859                 :            :                 {

     861                 :            :

     865                 :            :                          * pointer address to a Datum.

     878                 :      15624 :                         written_pos += real_size;

    1012                 :            : /*
    1013                 :            :  * deserializeValue -- deserialize a Datum value from a given char array.
    1014                 :            :  *
    1015                 :            :  * This function reads the char array as specified by 'read_pos', and
    1016                 :            :  * deserialize one serialized Datum values in this array into a given
    1017                 :            :  * WindowValue. If a Datum is passed by reference, this function does _not_
    1018                 :            :  * make a copy of this Datum, but simply set the pointer to the
    1019                 :            :  * corresponding address of the given char array.
    1020                 :            :  *
    1021                 :            :  * The return pointer points to the char position in the given char array
    1022                 :            :  * after reading one seralized string of WindowValue.
    1023                 :            :  *
    1024                 :            :  * Note that 'value' is allocated by the caller.
    1025                 :            :  */

    1039                 :     190306 :                 }

    1050                 :            :

    1052                 :            :                 }
    1053                 :     243283 :         }

    1306                 :            : /*

    1683                 :            : /*

    1691                 :            : hasTuplesInFrame(WindowStatePerLevel level_state,

    2112                 :            : /*
    2113                 :            :  * computeFrameValue -- compute transition values for each function
    2114                 :            :  * in a given key level from the data stored in its frame buffer.
    2115                 :            :  *
    2116                 :            :  * The 'trail_reader' and 'lead_reader' point to the positions of
    2117                 :            :  * the trailing and leading edges.
    2118                 :            :  */
    2119                 :            : static void
    2120                 :            : computeFrameValue(WindowStatePerLevel level_state,
    2121                 :            :                                   WindowState * wstate,
    2122                 :            :                                   NTupleStoreAccessor * trail_reader,
    2123                 :            :                                   NTupleStoreAccessor * lead_reader)
    2124                 :            : {
    2125                 :            :         ListCell   *lc;
    2126                 :      52032 :         ExprContext *econtext = wstate->ps.ps_ExprContext;
    2127                 :      52032 :         WindowFrameBuffer buffer = level_state->frame_buffer;
    2128                 :            :         FunctionCallInfoData fcinfo;
    2129                 :            : 
    2130                 :      52032 :         FrameBufferEntry *trail_entry = level_state->trail_entry_buf;
    2131                 :      52032 :         FrameBufferEntry *lead_entry = level_state->lead_entry_buf;
    2132                 :      52032 :         FrameBufferEntry *curr_entry = level_state->curr_entry_buf;
    2133                 :      52032 :         bool            has_trail_entry = false;
    2134                 :      52032 :         bool            has_lead_entry = false;
    2135                 :      52032 :         bool            has_curr_entry = false;

    2836                 :            : /*
    2837                 :            :  * Initialize WindowStatePerLevel in the Window node.

    2839                 :            : static void
    2840                 :            : initWindowStatePerLevel(WindowState * wstate, Window * node)
    2841                 :            : {
    2842                 :            :         ListCell   *lc;
    2843                 :      16581 :         int                     level_no = 0;
    2844                 :      16581 :         WindowKey  *prev_key = NULL;

    2850         [ +  + ]:      33420 :         foreach(lc, node->windowKeys)
    2851                 :            :         {
    2852                 :      16839 :                 WindowKey  *key = (WindowKey *) lfirst(lc);
    2853                 :            :                 WindowFrame *frame;
    2854                 :      16839 :                 WindowStatePerLevel lvl = &wstate->level_state[level_no++];
    2855                 :            :                 Oid                *eqOperators;

    2858                 :      16839 :                 lvl->has_delay_bound = false;
    2859                 :      16839 :                 lvl->has_only_trans_funcs = false;
    2860                 :            : 
    2861                 :            :                 /*
    2862                 :            :                  * Copy the sort columns for this level. If the window key contains an
    2863                 :            :                  * empty list of sort columns, we copy the previous non-empty list of
    2864                 :            :                  * sort columns here. This makes it easier to find keys to be stored
    2865                 :            :                  * in the frame buffer later.
    2866                 :            :                  */
    2867                 :      16839 :                 lvl->numSortCols = key->numSortCols;
    2868 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:      16839 :                 if (lvl->numSortCols == 0 && prev_key != NULL)
    2869                 :          0 :                         lvl->numSortCols += prev_key->numSortCols;
    2870                 :      16839 :                 lvl->sortColIdx = (AttrNumber *)palloc0(lvl->numSortCols * sizeof(AttrNumber));
    2871                 :      16839 :                 lvl->sortOperators = (Oid *) palloc0(lvl->numSortCols * sizeof(Oid));
    2872                 :            : 
    2873         [ -  + ]:      16839 :                 if (lvl->numSortCols > key->numSortCols)
    2874                 :            :                 {
    2875                 :          0 :                         memcpy(lvl->sortColIdx, prev_key->sortColIdx,
    2876                 :            :                                    prev_key->numSortCols * sizeof(AttrNumber));
    2877                 :          0 :                         memcpy(lvl->sortOperators, prev_key->sortOperators,
    2878                 :            :                                    prev_key->numSortCols * sizeof(Oid));
    2879                 :          0 :                 }
    2880                 :            : 
    2881                 :      16839 :                 memcpy(lvl->sortColIdx + (lvl->numSortCols - key->numSortCols),
    2882                 :            :                            key->sortColIdx,
    2883                 :            :                            key->numSortCols * sizeof(AttrNumber));
    2884                 :      16839 :                 memcpy(lvl->sortOperators + (lvl->numSortCols - key->numSortCols),
    2885                 :            :                            key->sortOperators,
    2886                 :            :                            key->numSortCols * sizeof(Oid));
    2887                 :            : 
    2888         [ +  + ]:      16839 :                 if (key->numSortCols > 0)
    2889                 :      15907 :                         prev_key = key;
    2890                 :            : 
    2891                 :            :                 /* Find comparison functions */
    2892                 :      16839 :                 eqOperators = (Oid *) palloc(lvl->numSortCols * sizeof(Oid));
    2893         [ +  + ]:      41079 :                 for (i = 0; i < lvl->numSortCols; i++)
    2894                 :            :                 {
    2895                 :      24240 :                         eqOperators[i] = get_equality_op_for_ordering_op(lvl->sortOperators[i]);
    2896                 :      24240 :                 }

    2898                 :      16839 :                                                                                                   eqOperators);

    2900                 :      16839 :                                                                            lvl->sortOperators);
    2901                 :      16839 :                 pfree(eqOperators);
    2902                 :            : 
    2903                 :            :                 /* Set the frame for this level */
    2904                 :      16839 :                 lvl->is_rows = false;
    2905                 :            : 
    2906         [ +  + ]:      16839 :                 if (!key->frame)
    2907                 :            :                 {
    2908                 :            :                         /*
    2909                 :            :                          * User didn't specify a frame, so we add the default. We do not
    2910                 :            :                          * do this in the parser because otherwise we cannot handle
    2911                 :            :                          * situation such as the following query being turned into a view:
    2912                 :            :                          *
    2913                 :            :                          * SELECT sum(...) OVER (w1), RANK() OVER (w1) ... WINDOW w1 AS
    2914                 :            :                          * (ORDER BY foo);
    2915                 :            :                          *
    2916                 :            :                          * When transformed to a view, we'd add the default framing clause
    2917                 :            :                          * and then the parser would barf when it encountered a window
    2918                 :            :                          * clause for a RANK function. So, we do it here.
    2919                 :            :                          */
    2920                 :            : 
    2921                 :       5933 :                         lvl->default_frame = true;
    2922                 :            : 
    2923 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       5933 :                         frame = (WindowFrame *) makeNode(WindowFrame);
    2924 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       5933 :                         frame->trail = (WindowFrameEdge *) makeNode(WindowFrameEdge);
    2925 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       5933 :                         frame->lead = (WindowFrameEdge *) makeNode(WindowFrameEdge);
    2926                 :            : 
    2927                 :            :                         /*
    2928                 :            :                          * The default frame is:
    2929                 :            :                          *
    2930                 :            :                          * RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    2931                 :            :                          */
    2932                 :       5933 :                         frame->is_rows = false;
    2933                 :       5933 :                         frame->trail->kind = WINDOW_UNBOUND_PRECEDING;
    2934                 :       5933 :                         frame->lead->kind = WINDOW_CURRENT_ROW;
    2935                 :            : 
    2936                 :       5933 :                         lvl->is_rows = false;
    2937                 :            : 
    2938                 :            :                         /*
    2939                 :            :                          * Note: we haven't set is_rows or exclude but the use of
    2940                 :            :                          * palloc0() inside of makeNode() ensures all fields of the struct

    3184                 :            : 
    3185                 :            : /* Advance the "primitive" values that drive the calculation of window
    3186                 :            :  * functions for the window ordering of the specified level.
    3187                 :            :  *
    3188                 :            :  * On entry these values are as set by partition initialization or as
    3189                 :            :  * left by the  previous call to this function. Per-partition state
    3190                 :            :  * has not yet been advanced by function nextRow(), our caller.
    3191                 :            :  *
    3192                 :            :  * The "primitive" values managed here are read-only outside the window
    3193                 :            :  * framework implemented by this function, initializePartition(), and
    3194                 :            :  * nextRow().
    3195                 :            :  *
    3196                 :            :  * Note that the content of different key levels is independent.  The
    3197                 :            :  * function nextRow() is responsible for calling this function as needed
    3198                 :            :  * for related key levels.
    3199                 :            :  */
    3200                 :            : 
    3201                 :            : static void
    3202                 :            : advanceKeyLevelState(WindowState * wstate, int min_level)
    3203                 :            : {
    3204                 :            :         WindowStatePerLevel level_state;
    3205                 :            :         int64           cur_row_index;
    3206                 :            :         int                     i;
    3207                 :            : 
    3208 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:      51743 :         Assert(-1 <= min_level && min_level < wstate->numlevels);
                 [ -  + ]
    3209                 :            : 
    3210                 :      51743 :         cur_row_index = wstate->row_index;
    3211                 :            : 
    3212         [ +  + ]:     104380 :         for (i = 0; i < wstate->numlevels; i++)
    3213                 :            :         {
    3214                 :      52637 :                 level_state = &wstate->level_state[i];
    3215                 :            : 
    3216 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:      52637 :                 if (i >= min_level && min_level != -1)
    3217                 :            :                 {
    3218                 :            :                         /*
    3219                 :            :                          * The level data is still set for the previous row. Remember the
    3220                 :            :                          * rank and dense_rank values.
    3221                 :            :                          */
    3222                 :      52623 :                         level_state->prior_rank = level_state->rank;
    3223                 :      52623 :                         level_state->prior_dense_rank = level_state->dense_rank;
    3224                 :            : 
    3225                 :            :                         /* Advance level data for first peer in next group. */
    3226                 :      52623 :                         level_state->group_index++;
    3227                 :      52623 :                         level_state->rank = 1 + cur_row_index;
    3228                 :      52623 :                         level_state->dense_rank = 1 + level_state->group_index;
    3229                 :      52623 :                 }
    3230                 :      52637 :         }
    3231                 :      51743 : }

    3415                 :            : /*
    3416                 :            :  * cmp_deformed_tuple -- compare two given arrays of Datums.
    3417                 :            :  *
    3418                 :            :  * If is_equal is true, this function returns true when these two Datums are
    3419                 :            :  * equal.
    3420                 :            :  *
    3421                 :            :  * If is_equal is false, this function return true if Datum a is ordered
    3422                 :            :  * before Datum b. For DESC ordering, 'ltFuncs' are actually > operators.
    3423                 :            :  */
    3424                 :            : static bool
    3425                 :            : cmp_deformed_tuple(Datum *a, bool *a_nulls, Datum *b, bool *b_nulls,
    3426                 :            :                                    int ncols, FmgrInfo *ltfuncs,
    3427                 :            :                                  FmgrInfo *eqfuncs, MemoryContext evalContext, bool is_equal)
    3428                 :            : {
    3429                 :            :         MemoryContext oldContext;
    3430                 :            :         bool            result;
    3431                 :            :         int                     i         ,
    3432                 :            :                                 j;
    3433                 :            :         FmgrInfo   *funcs;
    3434                 :            : 
    3435                 :            :         /* Reset and switch into the temp context. */
    3436                 :      52256 :         MemoryContextReset(evalContext);
    3437                 :      52256 :         oldContext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(evalContext);
    3438                 :            : 
    3439         [ +  + ]:      52256 :         if (is_equal)
    3440                 :            :         {
    3441                 :      14458 :                 funcs = eqfuncs;
    3442                 :      14458 :                 result = true;
    3443                 :      14458 :         }
    3444                 :            :         else
    3445                 :            :         {
    3446                 :      37798 :                 funcs = ltfuncs;
    3447                 :      37798 :                 result = true;
    3448                 :            :         }
    3449                 :            : 
    3450                 :            :         /*
    3451                 :            :          * For equality testing, we can will most likely find an inequality most
    3452                 :            :          * quickly by working from right to left. But, we might not be doing
    3453                 :            :          * equality testing in which case we must move from left to right.
    3454                 :            :          */
    3455         [ +  + ]:      52256 :         if (is_equal)
    3456                 :      14458 :                 i = ncols - 1;
    3457                 :            :         else
    3458                 :      37798 :                 i = 0;
    3459                 :            : 
    3460 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      56886 :         for (j = 0; j < ncols; j++, is_equal ? i-- : i++)
    3461                 :            :         {
    3462                 :            :                 Datum           attr1,
    3463                 :            :                                         attr2;
    3464                 :            :                 bool            isNull1,
    3465                 :            :                                         isNull2;
    3466                 :            :                 bool            res;
    3467                 :            : 
    3468                 :      52256 :                 attr1 = a[i];
    3469                 :      52256 :                 isNull1 = a_nulls[i];
    3470                 :            : 
    3471                 :      52256 :                 attr2 = b[i];
    3472                 :      52256 :                 isNull2 = b_nulls[i];
    3473                 :            : 
    3474         [ +  + ]:      52256 :                 if (isNull1 != isNull2)
    3475                 :            :                 {
    3476         [ -  + ]:          4 :                         if (is_equal)
    3477                 :            :                         {
    3478                 :          0 :                                 result = false; /* one null and one not; they aren't equal */
    3479                 :          0 :                                 break;
    3480                 :            :                         }
    3481                 :            :                         else
    3482                 :            :                         {
    3483                 :            :                                 /*
    3484                 :            :                                  * Currently, we assume NULLS LAST. In the future, when we add
    3485                 :            :                                  * support NULLS first to the window order-by clause, we
    3486                 :            :                                  * should take care of NULLS FIRST as well.
    3487                 :            :                                  */
    3488         [ -  + ]:          4 :                                 if (isNull1)
    3489                 :          0 :                                         result = false;
    3490                 :            :                                 else
    3491                 :          4 :                                         result = true;
    3492                 :          4 :                                 break;
    3493                 :            :                         }
    3494                 :            :                 }
    3495                 :            : 
    3496         [ +  + ]:      52252 :                 else if (isNull1)
    3497                 :          4 :                         continue;                       /* both are null, treat as equal */
    3498                 :            : 
    3499                 :            :                 /* Apply the type-specific function */
    3500                 :      52248 :                 res = DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2(&funcs[i], attr1, attr2));
    3501                 :            : 
    3502 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      52248 :                 if (is_equal && !res)
    3503                 :            :                 {
    3504                 :            :                         /* we wanted equality but didn't get it */
    3505                 :      12267 :                         result = false;
    3506                 :      12267 :                         break;
    3507                 :            :                 }
    3508         [ +  + ]:      39981 :                 else if (!is_equal)
    3509                 :            :                 {
    3510         [ +  + ]:      37792 :                         if (!res)
    3511                 :            :                         {
    3512                 :            :                                 /*
    3513                 :            :                                  * We wanted it to be less, but it wasn't. Continue only if
    3514                 :            :                                  * they're equal.
    3515                 :            :                                  */
    3516                 :      12587 :                                 res = DatumGetBool(FunctionCall2(&eqfuncs[i], attr1, attr2));
    3517         [ +  + ]:      12587 :                                 if (res)
    3518                 :            :                                 {
    3519                 :            :                                         /*
    3520                 :            :                                          * Because we found the previous key equal, set the return
    3521                 :            :                                          * value to false.
    3522                 :            :                                          */
    3523                 :       2437 :                                         result = false;
    3524                 :       2437 :                                         continue;
    3525                 :            :                                 }
    3526                 :            :                                 else
    3527                 :            :                                         /* LHS is greater than RHS */
    3528                 :      10150 :                                         result = false;
    3529                 :      10150 :                         }
    3530                 :            :                         else
    3531                 :            :                         {
    3532                 :            :                                 /* because all keys are sorted, we return straight away */
    3533                 :      25205 :                                 result = true;
    3534                 :            :                         }
    3535                 :      35355 :                         break;
    3536                 :            :                 }
    3537                 :       2189 :         }
    3538                 :            : 
    3539                 :      52256 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldContext);
    3540                 :            : 
    3541                 :      52256 :         return result;
    3542                 :            : 
    3543                 :            : }

    3545                 :            : /*
    3546                 :            :  * Like heap_deform_tuple(), but we only extract those fields listed in
    3547                 :            :  * attnums.

    3738                 :            : /*
    3739                 :            :  * adjustEdgesForRange -- adjust both the trailing and leading edge
    3740                 :            :  * to the given frame buffer for its level state.
    3741                 :            :  *
    3742                 :            :  * This function advances the leading reader until it rests on the position
    3743                 :            :  * at the frame buffer after which all key values are greater than
    3744                 :            :  * the leading edge value. The trailing reader rests on the position
    3745                 :            :  * at the frame buffer which key value is the greatest value in the

    3856                 :            : static void

    3915                 :            : 
    3916                 :            : /*
    3917                 :            :  * We must evaluate functions in a 'frame aware' way.
    3918                 :            :  *
    3919                 :            :  * UNBOUNDED PRECEDING frame edges are managed by passing the transition value
    3920                 :            :  * from the last call to the function. In this way, we trivially evaluate
    3921                 :            :  * all rows in the frame which have already passed. It gets more complex
    3922                 :            :  * for RANGE UNBOUNDED PRECEDING frames because all peers must emit the same
    3923                 :            :  * value -- so, we remember this in our state.
    3924                 :            :  *
    3925                 :            :  * Bounded PRECEDING and FOLLOWING edges are harder. We have two cases.
    3926                 :            :  *
    3927                 :            :  * With ROWS bounded frames, we must detect our position from the current
    3928                 :            :  * rows. If we are earlier on in the tuple count than the frame edge, those
    3929                 :            :  * tuples must be removed from the the transition value using the inversion
    3930                 :            :  * method for the function. If one doesn't exist, we do something even more
    3931                 :            :  * complex. See below. Once in the frame, we only care about the leading
    3932                 :            :  * frame edge (it could be the current row or some rows from it). So, we must
    3933                 :            :  * be careful about that.
    3934                 :            :  *
    3935                 :            :  * With RANGE bounded frames, we determine the edge with the equality
    3936                 :            :  * function(s) of the key level. Before and after the frame edges, we do the
    3937                 :            :  * same as for ROWS frames but remember, we must emit the same value for
    3938                 :            :  * all peers.
    3939                 :            :  *
    3940                 :            :  * If inversion functions do exist for a function, we must evaluate the frame
    3941                 :            :  * every single time it moves.
    3942                 :            :  */
    3943                 :            : static void
    3944                 :            : invokeWindowFuncs(WindowState * wstate)
    3945                 :            : {
    3946                 :     271386 :         bool            found_complex_func = false;
    3947                 :            : 
    3948                 :     271386 :         invokeTrivialFuncs(wstate, &found_complex_func);
    3949                 :            : 
    3950                 :            :         /*
    3951                 :            :          * If there are no functions/frames which need non-trivial window
    3952                 :            :          * management, we're done.
    3953                 :            :          */
    3954         [ +  + ]:     271386 :         if (found_complex_func)
    3955                 :      50387 :                 invokeNonTrivialFuncs(wstate);
    3956                 :            : 
    3957                 :     271385 :         finaliseFuncs(wstate);
    3958                 :     271385 : }
    3959                 :            :

    3972                 :         83 :                 return true;

    5009                 :            : /*
    5010                 :            :  * processTupleSlot -- process a new tuple.
    5011                 :            :  *
    5012                 :            :  * This function passes the given tuple to each function in every
    5013                 :            :  * key level to compute an intermediate result.
    5014                 :            :  *
    5015                 :            :  * We only handle non-trivial window functions here.
    5016                 :            :  *
    5017                 :            :  * 'last_peer' indicates if the tuple to be inserted is the last tuple from
    5018                 :            :  * the input. It is used to determine if the trailing edge and leading edge
    5019                 :            :  * need to be adjusted.
    5020                 :            :  */
    5021                 :            : static void
    5022                 :            : processTupleSlot(WindowState * wstate, TupleTableSlot * slot, bool last_peer)
    5023                 :            : {
    5024                 :            :         int                     level;

    5418                 :            : /*
    5419                 :            :  * make_eq_exprstate
    5420                 :            :  * Given expr1 and expr2, make clase "expr1 = expr2".
    5421                 :            :  * In case the result types of expr1 and expr2 are different,
    5422                 :            :  * the cast is installed on expr2.
    5423                 :            :  */
    5424                 :            : static ExprState *
    5425                 :            : make_eq_exprstate(WindowState * wstate, Expr *expr1, Expr *expr2)

    5436                 :            :         {

    5439                 :       1620 :                                                                                           restype2,
    5440                 :       1620 :                                                                                           restype1,
    5441                 :       1620 :                                                                                           exprTypmod((Node *) expr2),
    5442                 :            :                                                                                           COERCION_EXPLICIT,

    5457                 :            : /*
    5458                 :            :  * exec_eq_exprstate

    5463                 :            : static bool
    5464                 :            : exec_eq_exprstate(WindowState * wstate, ExprState *eq_exprstate)

    5475                 :            :

    5478                 :      24629 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldctx);
    5479                 :            : 
    5480         [ +  + ]:      24629 :         return isnull ? false : result;
    5481                 :            : }

    5496                 :            :                          frame->lead->kind == WINDOW_UNBOUND_PRECEDING ||

         [ +  + ][ +  - ]

    5605         [ +  + ]:       3258 :                                          frame->lead->kind == WINDOW_CURRENT_ROW &&

    5609                 :       2394 :                         }
    5610                 :      19674 :                 }
    5611                 :            :

    5805                 :            :         /*
    5806                 :            :          * Allocate one FrameBufferEntry buffer for each level state, so that we

    5808                 :            :          * frame buffer.

    5810         [ +  + ]:      33404 :         for (level = 0; level < wstate->numlevels; level++)

    5815                 :      16831 :                 level_state->trail_entry_buf = createFrameBufferEntry(level_state);
    5816                 :      16831 :                 level_state->lead_entry_buf = createFrameBufferEntry(level_state);

    5819                 :            : 
    5820                 :            : static FmgrInfo *
    5821                 :            : get_ltfuncs(int numCols, Oid *ltOperators)
    5822                 :            : {
    5823                 :      16839 :         FmgrInfo   *ltfunctions = (FmgrInfo *)palloc(numCols * sizeof(FmgrInfo));
    5824                 :            :         int                     i;
    5825                 :            : 
    5826         [ +  + ]:      41079 :         for (i = 0; i < numCols; i++)
    5827                 :            :         {
    5828                 :            :                 Oid                     lt_function;
    5829                 :            : 
    5830                 :      24240 :                 lt_function = get_opcode(ltOperators[i]);
    5831                 :      24240 :                 fmgr_info(lt_function, &ltfunctions[i]);
    5832                 :      24240 :         }
    5833                 :            : 
    5834                 :      16839 :         return ltfunctions;
    5835                 :            : }
    5836                 :            : 
    5837                 :            : /*
    5838                 :            :  * The buffer cursor abstracts a scan on the frame.  The tricky part is that
    5839                 :            :  * the underlying tuplestore may or may not have rows while in some circumstances
    5840                 :            :  * we should look at the input buffer as the last row, which is called "last_agg."
    5841                 :            :  * The usage of these APIs is straightforward; BeginScan, Next, EndScan.  Don't
    5842                 :            :  * open multiple cursors at one time on the same window level, as the cursor
    5843                 :            :  * has a transient tuplestore reader.
    5844                 :            :  * The forward parameter indicates if this cursor starts at the beginning and scans
    5845                 :            :  * forward, or if it starts at the end of frame and scans backward.
    5846                 :            :  */

    5848                 :            : windowBufferBeginScan(WindowState * wstate, WindowStatePerLevel level_state,

    5855                 :            : 
    5856                 :            :         /*
    5857                 :            :          * Because it is a short-term use, we don't allocate a new accessor but
    5858                 :            :          * use the existing one to scan the tuplestore.  The other side of reader
    5859                 :            :          * should remain so that detection of frame end can work correctly (i.e.
    5860                 :            :          * if both reader intersects, it means the end of frame.)

    5869                 :      10136 :         }
    5870                 :            :         else
    5871                 :            :         {
    5872                 :          0 :                 (void)ntuplestore_acc_tell(level_state->lead_reader,
    5873                 :          0 :                                                                    &cursor->orig_pos);
    5874                 :          0 :                 cursor->reader = level_state->lead_reader;
    5875                 :            :         }
    5876                 :            : 
    5877                 :            :         /*
    5878                 :            :          * This is a little complicated condition to tell if the last_agg row
    5879                 :            :          * should be looked at.  After setting use_last_agg, we'll return last_agg

    5881                 :            :          */

                 [ #  # ]

         [ +  + ][ +  - ]
                 [ #  # ]
    5885 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:      10136 :                 EDGE_IS_BOUND_FOLLOWING(level_state->frame->lead) ||
    5886 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:       5539 :         EDGE_EQ_CURRENT_ROW(level_state, wstate, level_state->frame->lead, true))
         [ +  - ][ +  + ]

    5939                 :            : /*
    5940                 :            :  * A helper of windowBufferNext.  Scans and advances in the tuplestore.
    5941                 :            :  * If it has reached to the end, sets reader_eof to true and returns NULL.
    5942                 :            :  * Otherwise returns a valid List containing serialized WindowValues.

    5945                 :            : windowBufferNextTupleStore(WindowBufferCursor cursor)

    5959                 :            :                         /*

    5967                 :       1864 :                                                                                           cursor->reader))
    5968                 :          0 :                                         cursor->reader_eof = true;
    5969                 :       1864 :                         }

    5971                 :            :                         {
    5972 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (ntuplestore_acc_tell(level_state->trail_reader, NULL) &&
    5973                 :          0 :                                         ntuplestore_acc_is_before(cursor->reader,
    5974                 :          0 :                                                                                           level_state->trail_reader))

    5998                 :            : /*
    5999                 :            :  * A helper of windowBufferNext.  Scans "last_agg" if we should, and converts the
    6000                 :            :  * input buffer to a List of WindowValues.      Since last_agg is the last row of
    6001                 :            :  * the frame if any, we set the use_last_agg to false after scanning and will never
    6002                 :            :  * get the values again.
    6003                 :            :  */

    6005                 :            : windowBufferNextLastAgg(WindowBufferCursor cursor)

    6011                 :       1389 :                 FrameBufferEntry *entry = level_state->curr_entry_buf;

    6246                 :            : /**
    6247                 :            :  * ExecReScanWindow
    6248                 :            :  *
    6249                 :            :  * Higher-up node is telling window node to perform re-scanning.
    6250                 :            :  * Note that exprCtxt may be NULL.
    6251                 :            :  */
    6252                 :            : void
    6253                 :            : ExecReScanWindow(WindowState * node, ExprContext *exprCtxt)
    6254                 :            : {
    6255 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         20 :         Assert(node);

    6257                 :         20 :         resetFrameBuffers(node);
    6258                 :            : 
    6259                 :         20 :         ExecEagerFreeWindow(node);
    6260                 :            : 
    6261                 :         20 :         node->is_input_done = false;
    6262                 :            : 
    6263 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         20 :         Assert(outerPlanState(node));
    6264                 :            : 
    6265                 :         20 :         ExecReScan(outerPlanState(node), exprCtxt);
    6266                 :         20 : }
    6267                 :            : 
    6268                 :            : /*
    6269                 :            :  * window_dummy - dummy execution routine for window functions
    6270                 :            :  *
    6271                 :            :  * This function is listed as the implementation (prosrc field) of pg_proc
    6272                 :            :  * entries for window functions.  Its only purpose is to throw an error
    6273                 :            :  * if someone mistakenly executes such a function in the normal way.

    6476                 :            : /*
    6477                 :            :  *      NTILE(BIGINT) OVER (... ORDER BY s) --> BIGINT
    6478                 :            :  *
    6479                 :            :  * Implement NTILE for the given WindowRefExprState.
    6480                 :            :  *
    6481                 :            :  * ntile_prelim_int(internal,int) --> bigint[]
    6482                 :            :  * ntile_prelim_bigint(internal,bigint) --> bigint[]

    6486                 :            : 
    6487                 :            : /* Helper. */
    6488                 :            : static ArrayType *
    6489                 :            : do_ntile_prelim(WindowRefExprState * ref_state, int64 num_tiles)
    6490                 :            : {
    6491                 :            :         /*
    6492                 :            :          * Pack row_index from state and the argument num_tiles into a two-element
    6493                 :            :          * array of type bigint.
    6494                 :            :          */
    6495                 :            : 
    6496                 :            :         Datum           work  [2];
    6497                 :            : 
    6498                 :        190 :         WindowState *window_state = ref_state->windowstate;
    6499                 :            : 
    6500         [ +  + ]:        190 :         if (num_tiles <= 0)
    6501         [ +  - ]:          4 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    6502                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_NUMERIC_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE),
    6503                 :            :                                  errmsg("argument value out of range"),
    6504                 :            :                                  errhint("NTILE expects a positive integer argument.")));
    6505                 :            : 
    6506                 :        186 :         work[0] = Int64GetDatumFast(num_tiles);         /* ntile argument */
    6507                 :        186 :         work[1] = Int64GetDatumFast(window_state->row_index);
    6508                 :            : 
    6509                 :        186 :         return construct_array(work, 2, INT8OID, 8, true, 'd');
    6510                 :            : }
    6511                 :            : 
    6512                 :            : Datum
    6513                 :            : ntile_prelim_int(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
    6514                 :            : {
    6515                 :        190 :         WindowRefExprState *rstate = (WindowRefExprState *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(0);
    6516                 :        190 :         int64           ntile_arg = (int64) PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
    6517                 :            : 
    6518                 :        190 :         PG_RETURN_ARRAYTYPE_P(do_ntile_prelim(rstate, ntile_arg));
    6519                 :            : }
